title: isocoma gypsophilia
intro: Isocoma gypsophila, also known as White Bush Clover, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Isocoma gypsophila include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, colds, and digestive problems.
